seo_title: New AI Technique Makes Models Leaner and Faster During Training
meta_description: A new technique called CompreSSM makes AI models more efficient by removing unnecessary complexity during training, cutting compute costs without sacrificing…
body_html:
Researchers have developed a new technique called CompreSSM that makes AI models more efficient by removing unnecessary complexity during training.

The technique uses mathematical tools from control theory to identify which parts of a model are pulling their weight and which are dead weight. The team shows that the relative importance of different components within these models stabilizes surprisingly early during training, allowing them to reliably rank which dimensions matter and which don't after only about 10 percent of the training process.

Reduced compute costs Improved model performance

Increased efficiency during training Ability to remove unnecessary complexity

Future Directions "It's essentially a technique to make models grow smaller and faster as they are training," says Makram Chahine, a PhD student in electrical engineering and computer science.

Read more about this breakthrough in AI research at MIT News.

source.title: New technique makes AI models leaner and faster while they’re still learning
source.summary: Researchers use control theory to shed unnecessary complexity from AI models during training, cutting compute costs without sacrificing performance.
